# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 112

This is turn 112. Turns 1 through 111 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 112.

## Turn 112 — standalone \(\partial\)-framed exact-image theorem

The candidate remains unchanged. The tame relation is implicit, \(x_0,x_1\) are constrained to the \(2\)-core, and the displayed exponent is the verifier residue of the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega_2\). 

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

One terminology correction from Turn 111: the active-block proposition below uses the **direct Frattini–centralizer collapse**, not Thompson criticality.

### 1. Common boundary

Let \(T\) be the common tame quotient and let

\[
\Pi=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\;\middle|\;
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}
\]

be the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient. Put

\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}=\Pi/\Phi(\Pi)\cong C_2^3.
\]

Both \(T\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\) have a distinguished quotient \(C_2\), recording the parity of \(\sigma\). Define

\[
\partial=T\times_{C_2}\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}.
\]

For

\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,\Gamma_F\},\qquad
\Gamma_F=G_{\mathbb Q_2},
\]

the tame and elementary pro-\(2\) maps combine to an epimorphism

\[
b_\Gamma:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow\partial.
\]

Surjectivity follows from profinite Goursat: every common quotient of \(T\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\) is an elementary \(2\)-quotient of \(T\), hence at most \(C_2\), while the common unramified quadratic character shows that it is exactly \(C_2\). This is the common boundary construction reached in Turn 111. 

Fix maps

\[
\alpha:T\twoheadrightarrow H,
\qquad
\bar\psi:\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\to E,
\]

with \(E\) elementary abelian, and put

\[
\beta:\partial\to H\times E,
\qquad
\beta(t,p)=(\alpha(t),\bar\psi(p)).
\]

A \(\partial\)-framed marked target is a finite group \(Y\) equipped with

\[
L_Y\triangleleft Y,\qquad L_Y\ \text{a \(2\)-group},
\qquad
\pi_Y:Y\twoheadrightarrow H,\quad\ker\pi_Y=L_Y,
\qquad
\theta_Y:Y\to E.
\]

Write \(q_Y=(\pi_Y,\theta_Y)\). Define

\[
e_\Gamma^\beta(Y)=
\#\{f:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow Y:\ q_Yf=\beta b_\Gamma\}.
\tag{112.1}
\]

Since the wild subgroup is \(\ker(t_\Gamma)\), equation (112.1) automatically forces its image into \(L_Y\).

### 2. Six propositions

#### Proposition 1 — terminal scalar pullback

If every \(Y\)-chief factor contained in \(L_Y\) is trivial, then

\[
Y\cong H\times_{H_2}Q
\]

for a finite \(2\)-group \(Q\), where \(H_2\) is the maximal \(2\)-quotient of \(H\).

The odd kernel of \(H\to H_2\) is killed by every map to \(E\), so \(\theta_Y\) factors through \(Q\). Boundary-framed maps to \(Y\), including every exact-image stratum, are consequently identical to compatible maps from the common group \(\Pi\) to subgroups of \(Q\). Hence the candidate and local counts agree literally in this branch.

#### Proposition 2 — direct active-block collapse

Suppose the terminal hypothesis fails. Choose the first nontrivial simple chief factor

\[
P/S\cong V
\]

above the scalar chief run, and choose \(K\triangleleft Y\), minimal with \(K\le P\) and \(KS=P\). Put

\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad
M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R,\qquad
C=Y/K,\qquad
B=Y/R.
\]

The direct Frattini–centralizer argument gives

\[
R\le Z(K),\qquad K^4=1,
\]

so \(R\) is elementary abelian. Minimality gives

\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbb F_2[C]}(M),
\qquad
M/T_0\cong V,
\qquad
(M^\vee)^C=0.
\tag{112.2}
\]

Moreover, \(K\le\ker q_Y\). Indeed, \(\theta_Y(R)=0\), while \(\theta_Y|_K\) factors through a \(C\)-map \(M\to E\), which vanishes by \((M^\vee)^C=0\). Thus quotienting by \(K\) or \(R\) preserves the same boundary equation.

#### Proposition 3 — common-word elementary duality

For an exact lower map \(\rho:\Gamma_A\to C\) and any elementary \(\mathbb F_2[C]\)-module \(A\), the candidate relation-deformation complex is

\[
0\longrightarrow A\longrightarrow A^4\longrightarrow A^2\longrightarrow0.
\]

Finite-Heisenberg evaluation of the same two relators gives a natural perfect duality

\[
H_\rho^i(A)\times H_\rho^{2-i}(A^\vee)\longrightarrow\mathbb F_2.
\]

Consequently,

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(A)^\vee\cong(A^\vee)^C,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(A)
=
2\dim A+\dim(A^\vee)^C.
\tag{112.3}
\]

The local source has the identical formulas by local Tate duality and Euler characteristic. Because \(A\le K\le\ker q_Y\), the boundary condition does not cut either lift torsor.

For \(A=M\), equation (112.2) gives

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)=0,
\qquad
|Z_\rho^1(M)|=2^{2\dim M}.
\tag{112.4}
\]

#### Proposition 4 — simple-head reduction and shearing

For

\[
0\ne\lambda\in (R^\vee)^B=(R^\vee)^C,
\]

push out \(R\) along \(\lambda:R\to C_2\). The resulting quadratic map

\[
q_\lambda:M\to\mathbb F_2
\]

satisfies

\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0,
\qquad
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0,
\]

and therefore descends to a nonzero nonsingular invariant form

\[
\bar q_\lambda:V\to\mathbb F_2.
\tag{112.5}
\]

If the filtration-one edge across \(T_0\) is nonzero, the obstruction is a nonzero affine functional under Proposition 3, so exactly half of the unrestricted lift torsor survives.

If the edge vanishes, the class inflates past \(T_0\). The transgression formula

\[
d_2(\bar q_\lambda)
=
b_{\bar q_\lambda}^{\flat}(\eta)
\]

forces the exposed \(V\)-extension to split. A \(C\)-equivariant shear removes the remaining \(V^\vee\)-edge and changes only an inflated class

\[
\delta\in H^2(C,\mathbb F_2).
\]

Every shear and pushout occurs inside \(K\le\ker q_Y\), so the full boundary frame is preserved.

#### Proposition 5 — determinant zero counts

Let \(d=\dim V\). The candidate determinant obstruction is

\[
Q_A(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\qquad U=S^{\omega_2}.
\]

The Wall calculation and the local Kummer–Hilbert calculation give the same zero counts:

\[
N_0=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\ \text{unramified},\\[2mm]
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{112.6}
\]

Thus the pure determinant fibre depends only on the fixed tame action on \(V\), hence only on the fixed boundary stratum—not on the lower wild map.

#### Proposition 6 — boundary-safe central-cover transform

Let

\[
p:\widetilde Y\twoheadrightarrow Y
\]

be a marked central \(C_2\)-cover and give \(\widetilde Y\) the pulled-back boundary map \(q_{\widetilde Y}=q_Yp\). For an exact-image subgroup \(J\le Y\), let \(u_\Gamma^\beta(p,J)\) count boundary-framed exact-image maps to \(J\) whose pullback of \(p\) splits. Then

\[
8u_\Gamma^\beta(p,J)
=
\sum_{\substack{\widetilde J\le p^{-1}(J)\\p(\widetilde J)=J}}
e_\Gamma^\beta(\widetilde J).
\tag{112.7}
\]

Every unobstructed map has eight lifts because

\[
|\operatorname{Hom}(\Gamma,C_2)|
=
|\operatorname{Hom}(\Pi_{\mathrm{el}},C_2)|
=8.
\]

Twisting changes only the new central coordinate, which lies in \(\ker q_{\widetilde Y}\); hence all eight lifts preserve equation (112.1).

### 3. Boundary-framed theorem

**Theorem.** For every finite \(\partial\)-framed marked target \(Y\),

\[
e_{\Gamma_A}^{\beta}(Y)=e_{\Gamma_F}^{\beta}(Y).
\tag{112.8}
\]

**Proof.** Use strong induction on \(|L_Y|\).

The terminal scalar branch is Proposition 1.

Otherwise take the active block of Proposition 2. The quotient \(C=Y/K\) has strictly smaller marked kernel. Proposition 3 gives the same unrestricted \(M\)-lift multiplicity over every lower map. Every proper exact image in \(B=Y/R\) has strictly smaller marked kernel, so the global induction converts unrestricted equality into exact-image equality.

For the \(R\)-stage, set

\[
D=(R^\vee)^B.
\]

Fourier inversion reconstructs the zero fibre of the full \(R\)-valued obstruction from its scalar pushouts:

\[
\#\{\rho:o_\Gamma(\rho)=0\}
=
\frac1{|D|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D}
\bigl(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}-|X_\Gamma|\bigr).
\tag{112.9}
\]

The \(\lambda=0\) term is the already-matched \(B\)-count. For \(\lambda\ne0\), Proposition 4 reduces the pushout either to an exact half-fibre or to a pure determinant fibre plus an inflated scalar constant. Proposition 5 matches the determinant fibre, while Proposition 6 expresses both the weighted proper-image corrections and the inflated constant through ordinary exact-image counts in targets of strictly smaller marked-kernel order.

Thus every Fourier coefficient in (112.9) agrees.

Once the \(R\)-obstruction vanishes, Proposition 3 gives the same \(R\)-lift multiplicity. Every such lift is surjective: if its image maps onto \(Y/R\), then

\[
(J\cap K)R=K,
\]

and \(R=\Phi(K)\) forces \(J\cap K=K\), hence \(J=Y\). This proves (112.8). \(\square\)

Taking \(E=0\), summing over all tame frames \(\alpha\), and setting \(L_Y=O_2(Y)\) gives the official verifier semantics:

\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_A,Y)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbb Q_2},Y)|
\]

for every finite \(Y\), provided the six propositions withstand independent manuscript review. The attached history’s boundary formulation and six-lemma audit are the direct antecedents of this theorem. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Rewrote the full argument as one standalone \(\partial\)-framed exact-image theorem with six separately stated propositions. Corrected Turn 111’s obsolete “Thompson-critical” wording: the active block uses the direct Frattini–centralizer collapse.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the assembled theorem still needs an independent line-by-line review rather than another internal repackaging.

**NEXT**: Audit Proposition 3 at chain level and Proposition 5 at the local Shapiro/corestriction interface as the two highest-leverage remaining manuscript checks, then submit the unchanged residue block.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
